reviewer: build-wave agent (real headless-Chrome first-visit + cached-reload verification, revision 1)

rubric.evidence: Real first-visit run in headless Chrome (WASM, no-GPU): clicked Download (~570 MB), reached loader state=ready, then the page auto-embedded the default multilingual passages and rendered the cross-language cosine matrix. Standalone transformers.js@3.7.5 check returned dims [n,1024] and cross-lingual cosines EN↔ES 0.926, EN↔JA 0.915, EN↔unrelated 0.38 (CLS pooling). conformance (headless, no-GPU): 14✓ 0✗ 0▨ 9◍/23 — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in models/bge-m3-embeddings/worker.js via loadPipeline (feature-extraction, Xenova/bge-m3, q8, pooling:cls).

rubric.evidence: Output is a fixed-length 1024-d numeric vector per passage (dims confirmed [n,1024]); L2-normalized so cosine = dot product. Semantic sanity verified: same-meaning cross-lingual pairs 0.91–0.93, unrelated 0.38 — a clean separation. CLS pooling chosen over mean after measuring mean pooling left unrelated pairs at 0.61 (less discriminative).
rubric.notes: worker returns un-normalized CLS vectors + pre-norm magnitude + real per-text token counts so 'See inside' shows the 8192-window usage.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (Xenova/bge-m3) linked to card, declares-task (feature-extraction), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (new Worker) all pass — 5✓. At-a-glance also states the honest ~570 MB size and 8192-token context.

Cached-reload test (same profile, Cache Storage persists): the page AUTO-initialised to state=ready with NO Download click; the only action button shown was 'Clear cached model', and the matrix re-rendered automatically. shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader). Critical for a 570 MB model — a returning user never re-downloads.
rubric.notes: Uses the shared lib/model-loader.js auto-init architecture; no hand-rolled Load button. Per-model clear-cache control present.

rubric.evidence: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live on #rowStatus and the loader status), errors-on-the-page=pass (embed/search failures write to an .err status). Console clean on first visit AND reload (0 errors). runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only model — ran fully on the no-GPU runner.
rubric.notes: Download progress surfaced via the shared loader <progress>; the honest ~570 MB note sets expectations before the download.

rubric.evidence: Controls: passages textarea (labelled), Embed & Reset buttons, cross-lingual search input + button (Enter-key handled), the 'inspect passage' select, plus the loader Download/Clear buttons — all real, labelled, keyboard-operable. buttons-have-names=pass. Rendering verified desktop+mobile: reports/responsive/screens/bge-m3-embeddings/{desktop,mobile}.png.
rubric.notes: Ladder pages add k-means slider, dedup threshold slider, and a two-model retrieve→translate pipeline.

rubric.evidence: Every cosine, matrix cell, projection point and search rank is computed from the on-device 1024-d vectors; verified the numbers change with input and match the standalone cross-lingual measurements. No canned vectors or results in the source.
rubric.notes: PCA/k-means run in plain JS on the model's real vectors — the geometry, not a script, does the grouping.

rubric.evidence: images-have-alt=pass (no content images; the PCA SVG carries role=img + aria-label), buttons-have-names=pass. Matrix uses th scope=row/col; cell text colour switches to accent-ink above 0.6 for contrast. Live regions announce status. Light + dark both legible: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify-bge-m3-embeddings/{light,dark}.png.
rubric.notes: Design-system tokens throughout; matrix scrolls inside its own overflow-x container so the body never scrolls horizontally.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check (automated): desktop (1280×800) clean, mobile (360×740 DPR3) clean — no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console+network clean. Wide matrix confined to a .matrix-scroll overflow-x:auto container. Screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/bge-m3-embeddings/{desktop,mobile}.png. support recorded needs-review pending a deeper agent tap-target pass.
rubric.notes: grid-2 uses repeat(auto-fit,minmax(300px,1fr)) so the matrix and 2D map stack on mobile.

rubric.evidence: All embedding runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only runs the small PCA/k-means over a handful of already-computed vectors and renders DOM. No blocking loop; INP stays low even while embedding a batch of long passages.
rubric.notes: guidance break-up-long-tasks applied via the worker; long-doc embedding never blocks input.

guidanceConsulted.query: wide data table / similarity matrix that scrolls inside its own container without page overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Confine wide content to its own overflow-x:auto scroll container so the page body never scrolls horizontally; use grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax()) for the two-up matrix/map so it stacks on narrow viewports; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the cosine matrix lives in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto); the matrix+map use an auto-fit grid that stacks on mobile; verified no body overflow at 360px and 1280px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep the main thread responsive; move heavy work off-thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all BGE-M3 embedding (including long 8192-token passages and batches) runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only does cheap PCA/k-means on the returned vectors.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; console clean during live embedding.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce errors and status changes in an accessible live region next to the action.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite on the embed/search status lines and the loader status; failures add an .err class the user can read.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system, invariant 6).

openQuestions: Is ~570 MB (q8) acceptable on mid-range phones, or should the page proactively suggest the lighter multilingual MiniLM below a memory/network threshold?
Would exposing the sparse/multi-vector M3 heads (not just dense) be worth a follow-up demo, or does that overload the page?

followUpGoals.goal: Deeper mobile tap-target + focus-order audit on BGE-M3 (search input/button and the inspect select at 360px) to flip support from needs-review to ok with recorded evidence.
Add a BGE-M3 long-document chunk-vs-whole demo contrasting embedding a full section (8192 window) against sentence-chunking, to make the long-context advantage concrete.

summary: Revision-1 critique for bge-m3-embeddings (BGE-M3 dense head, feature-extraction, WASM q8), backed by a real headless first-visit download → live output run, cross-lingual semantic sanity (EN↔ES 0.926, EN↔JA 0.915, unrelated 0.38, dims 1024 via CLS pooling), a cached-reload auto-init check (no Download button, 'Clear cached model' offered — essential for a 570 MB model), conformance 14✓/0✗/9 manual, and a clean desktop+mobile responsive scan with the wide matrix confined to its own scroll container. Honest about the size trade-off; mobile tap-target depth left as a follow-up.
